name: Spinach and Olive Pie.
recipeInstructions:
Crust:
1. Cut the butter into the flour and work with your fingers until it looks like cornmeal.
2. Add the salt and mix well.
3. Beat the egg yolk into the ice water, then add to a well in the center of the flour mixture.
4. Work with the heel of your hand until smooth.
5. Form a ball, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for one hour.
Filling:
1. Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the onions and stir continually until soft and lightly browned (about 15 minutes).
3. Remove from heat and season with salt and pepper to taste.
4. Remove the dough from the refrigerator and roll out to make a thick (1/4") crust.
5. Place anchovy fillets in a star-shaped pattern along the bottom.
6. Cover with half the olives, then add half the onions.
7. Add the remaining olives and onions on top.
8. Place the pie in a 375°F oven and bake for 40 minutes to an hour until the crust is lightly browned and onions are brown.
From the Sunday NYT.
